title: Modules (import/export)
summary: Modules split your code into separate files.
export shares something from a file.
import brings it into another file.

sections.text: As a project grows, putting all your code in one file becomes a nightmare. Modules let you break it into many small files that share what they need, keeping everything tidy and easy to find.

sections.title: Export and Import
sections.text: A module is just a file. To share something from it, like a function, you export it. To use that something in another file, you import it. Export sends out, import brings in. Together they connect your files.

sections.title: Why Modules Help
sections.text: Modules keep each file focused on one job. You can find code faster, reuse pieces across the project, and avoid one giant tangled file. They also make it clear which file depends on which, so the structure of a project is easier to follow.

media.alt: One file exporting a function and another importing it

sections.title: A Library of Books
sections.text: Think of your project as a library. Each module is a book on one topic. Export is like publishing a chapter so others can read it. Import is like borrowing that chapter when you need it. Instead of one endless book, you have a neat shelf of focused ones.

media.alt: Books on a shelf, one lending a chapter to another

sections.title: Using Modules
items: Put related code in its own file.
Export the parts other files will need.
In another file, import what you want to use.
Use the imported piece as if it were local.
Keep each file focused on a single job.

sections.text: Browsers can now load modules directly, but most projects still use a build tool to bundle the many files into a few for speed. You write many, the browser gets few.

terms.definition: A single file of related code.
Shares something out of a file.
One document of code in your project.
Combining many files into fewer for speed.

options: Split your code into separate files
questions.explanation: Modules split your code into separate, focused files.
export shares something out of a file so other files can use it.
import brings something from another file into the current one.
